summary: Removed the unused Dolphin GX header `include/dolphin/gx/gxtypes.h`. The deleted file contained standalone GX type, enum, struct, and macro definitions such as `GXTexMtx`, `GXTexMapID`, `GXPTTexMtx`, `GXTexWrapMode`, `GXFifoObj`, `GXRenderModeObj`, `GXLightObj`, `GXColor`, `GXColorS10`, GX boolean aliases, GX vertex attribute constants, gamma constants, and light/Z constants. No discussion or review comments were present in the slice, so the motivation is limited to the PR title: the header was unused.
what_changed: Removed `GX_FIFO_OBJSIZE` and the `GXFifoObj` wrapper struct with `u8 pad[GX_FIFO_OBJSIZE]`.
Removed GX texture matrix and texture map enums: `GXTexMtx`, `GXTexMapID`, `GXPTTexMtx`, and `GXTexWrapMode`.
Removed render and light/color type declarations: `GXRenderModeObj`, `GXLightObj`, `GXColor`, and `GXColorS10`.
Removed macro constants for GX boolean values, vertex attributes, gamma modes, `GX_MAX_Z24`, and `MAX_GXLIGHT`.
smart_moves: Kept the cleanup minimal and isolated to a single unused header, reducing risk of behavioral or matching changes.
Removed duplicate or dead public-looking GX definitions rather than preserving unused API surface that could mislead future decompilation work.
The PR was small enough to be mechanically reviewable: one deleted file, no source edits, no semantic changes shown in the diff.
decomp_lessons: Unused SDK-style headers can accumulate stale or redundant type definitions; deleting them can improve include hygiene when no translation units depend on them.
Do not keep Dolphin/GX type definitions merely because they look canonical; verify whether the project actually includes the header.
For future GX decomp work, prefer the active headers already used by the build over reintroducing deleted standalone definitions from `gxtypes.h` unless a concrete dependency requires them.
Header cleanup PRs should be narrow and evidence-driven because removing type definitions is safe only if the build and include graph prove they are unused.
naming_conventions: Deleted header used Dolphin GX-style type names with `GX` prefixes, including `GXTexMtx`, `GXTexMapID`, `GXPTTexMtx`, `GXTexWrapMode`, `GXFifoObj`, `GXRenderModeObj`, `GXLightObj`, `GXColor`, and `GXColorS10`.
Deleted enum constants used all-caps SDK-style names such as `GX_TEXMTX0`, `GX_TEXMAP_NULL`, `GX_PTTEXMTX19`, `GX_CLAMP`, and `GX_MAX_TEXWRAPMODE`.
Deleted boolean and enable/disable macros used `GX_FALSE`, `GX_TRUE`, `GX_DISABLE`, and `GX_ENABLE`.
Deleted vertex attribute macros used `GX_VA_*` names, ending with sentinel-style values such as `GX_VA_MAXATTR` and `GX_VA_NULL`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or function-matching tactic is evidenced; this PR only removed an unused header.
The practical matching tactic is indirect: keep the header/type environment lean so future matching work does not accidentally rely on stale duplicate GX declarations.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: If GX type definitions are needed later, search for the active replacement header before reintroducing `include/dolphin/gx/gxtypes.h`.
Audit current includes for `dolphin/gx/gxtypes.h` if a branch or downstream fork fails after rebasing across PR 482.
Search for duplicate declarations of `GXRenderModeObj`, `GXColor`, `GXTexMapID`, and `GXTexMtx` in the remaining Dolphin GX headers.
